Sent: Tuesday, May 20, 2025 1:33 PM
To: <dpwillis67@gmail.com>
Subject: ACT NOW: Congress wants to greenlight violence against our clients
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075299/546783783/2141984067?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237) | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075300/546783784/2141984067?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237)
---|---
| Peter,

Just days after a deadly bombing outside a fertility clinic in California, members of Congress are pushing to repeal the Freedom of Access to Clinic Entrances (FACE) Act—[**the only federal law that protects reproductive health clinics and places of worship from violence and harassment.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075301/546783785/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237) | [**SAY NO**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075302/546783786/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237 "DONATE NOW")
---
The FACE Act was passed in 1994 after a wave of anti-abortion violence, including the murder of Dr. David Gunn outside a clinic in Florida. The law made threatening patients or providers, or blocking access to care, a federal crime. But since _Roe v. Wade_ was overturned, attacks have skyrocketed—including almost 300 death threats or threats of harm. [**Repealing the FACE Act would say loud and clear: violence against patients and providers is no longer a federal crime.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075303/546783787/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237)
[**TELL CONGRESS: NO REPEAL**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075304/546783788/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237 "DONATE NOW")
---
Earlier this year, President Trump pardoned multiple anti-abortion extremists convicted under the FACE Act. Some had broken into clinics, stolen fetal tissue, or harassed pregnant patients. [**Eight of them had targeted a Michigan clinic the Center for Reproductive Rights represents.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075305/546783789/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237)

These pardons sent a clear and dangerous message: [**this kind of targeted violence is acceptable.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075306/546783790/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237) Now Congress is trying to finish what he started by erasing the only federal protection clinics have left.

[**Repealing the FACE Act would endanger lives and embolden extremists. Demand your representative defend the FACE Act and protect people from violence.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108075307/546783791/734415106?sourceid=1150699&utm_campaign=25emeus-0503aax-onv&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=faceact&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTc1YmZkNi0zNDM2LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vZW-UX3bjNNNcd0pc7VBk0ihrRUGZheZGVjX-2nnHmk=&emci=31ad34e6-b835-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=fa75bfd6-3436-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237)

In solidarity,

Center for Reproductive Rights

clean_body: Attack on abortion access happening TODAY Dear Peter, I've been meeting with allies on Capitol Hill all week to ensure they know how dangerous repealing the FACE Act will be. We can't sit by and let this happen. In solidarity, Nancy Northup * From: Center for Reproductive Rights <info@reprorights.org> Sent: Tuesday, May 20, 2025 1:33 PM To: <dpwillis67@gmail.com> Subject: ACT NOW: Congress wants to greenlight violence against our clients | | ---|--- | Peter, Just days after a deadly bombing outside a fertility clinic in California, members of Congress are pushing to repeal the Freedom of Access to Clinic Entrances (FACE) Act— | "DONATE NOW" The FACE Act was passed in 1994 after a wave of anti-abortion violence, including the murder of Dr. David Gunn outside a clinic in Florida. The law made threatening patients or providers, or blocking access to care, a federal crime. But since Roe v. Wade was overturned, attacks have skyrocketed—including almost 300 death threats or threats of harm. "DONATE NOW" Earlier this year, President Trump pardoned multiple anti-abortion extremists convicted under the FACE Act. Some had broken into clinics, stolen fetal tissue, or harassed pregnant patients. These pardons sent a clear and dangerous message: Now Congress is trying to finish what he started by erasing the only federal protection clinics have left. In solidarity, Center for Reproductive Rights "DONATE NOW" | | | | ---|---|--- The Center for Reproductive Rights uses the power of law to advance reproductive rights as fundamental human rights around the world. © Center for Reproductive Rights Center for Reproductive Rights 199 Water St. New York, NY 10038 United States

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: LAST CHANCE: One, Big, Beautiful Bill Changes
body:
The House will vote on a final version of the One, Big, Beautiful Bill this week.
| | | |
---
|
---
| Patriot-

---
| **** We sent you an action item on Monday asking you to call your representative with an urgent request to make the following changes to the One, Big, Beautiful Bill.
* Start work requirements for Medicaid right now--don't wait until 2029.
* End the Green New Deal and Inflation "Reduction" Act spending that's killing our economy and serves as taxpayer-funded slush fund for leftwing groups.
* Remove the "free" money/new entitlement program--the $1,000 "MAGA Account" giveaway.
**While we can still try to make these changes in the U.S. Senate, it will likely be a more difficult fight in that chamber. The time to make these changes is _NOW._**
**ACTION:** **Call your representative right now!** Use the United States Capitol Switchboard at (202) 224-3121 and ask for your U.S. Representative. Don't know who your representative is? [**Use this website**](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ab2ea78e/1edb9794ab2de76a) to find out. Simply enter your zip code and your U.S. Representative will be listed. Then, call the switchboard and ask for your representative's office. Once you are connected, give your name and the city where you live, and then read those three bullet points above to the person who answers the phone. **********Please do not let this opportunity to make a real difference for our country pass you by. It 's one, quick phone call and it's so very important to our shared mission of America First!********** In liberty, Jenny Beth Martin **__****__****__****__** __ **__** **** ************

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 5/21/2025
body:
Cities sue Polis over housing mandate, cite threat to local control
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi folks!
Listen to today's **Mountain Minute Podcast** [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)! Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** Colorado’s soft-on-crime policies are under fire after a new report revealed violent offenders routinely commit new crimes [while out on bond](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). A Christian camp is [suing the state](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) over rules requiring access to gender identity–aligned facilities, calling it a violation of religious freedom.
Meanwhile, multiple cities—including Arvada and Aurora—are [suing Governor Polis](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) over new housing mandates they say override local authority. And from gun control to gray wolves and wild bison, a slate of new bills passed this session will [reshape operations at Colorado Parks and Wildlife](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Denver City Council is bracing for [major budget cuts](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—but Mayor Mike Johnston still hasn’t revealed how deep they’ll go.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** President Trump announced that Russia and Ukraine will begin immediate ceasefire negotiations following a [two-hour call with Vladimir Putin](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), with the Vatican offering to host peace talks. Meanwhile, a sweeping new pro-family proposal includes ["MAGA Baby Savings Accounts"](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—a 401(k)-style plan for newborns—as part of a larger push to boost the American birth rate and support young families.
**🗣️ Commentary Spotlight...** Cory Gaines blasts Colorado AG Phil Weiser for spending taxpayer money on [partisan lawsuits](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and gun control efforts. Meanwhile, Dr. Brian Joondeph praises Trump’s [revived drug pricing order](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), calling it a win for patients and a blow to global price disparities.
This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, May 21, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Cities sue Polis over housing mandate, cite threat to local control](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Six “home rule” cities in Colorado are suing the state, alleging it has unconstitutionally usurped their local authority over land use and zoning as it pushes communities to allow denser housing development.
The lawsuit challenges the constitutionality of an executive order Gov. Jared Polis signed last week to withhold some state grants from local communities if they fail to implement a slate of recent housing laws. The cities say the order encroaches on the powers of both the General Assembly and the judiciary to say what the law is and is “beyond the governor’s authority.”
The cities are also asking the courts to find they don’t have to follow two new laws approved by the legislature last year which require cities to allow denser housing near transit and to do away with minimum parking requirements for some new multi-family developments.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Out on bond, out of control: Decades of Colorado’s soft-on-crime policies under fire](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* A recent wave of violent crimes in Denver—committed by offenders out on low or no-cost bonds—has reignited debate over Colorado’s bail and sentencing policies.

* From a trooper nearly stabbed at the Capitol to a gas station shooting by a man with 50 prior arrests, public outrage is mounting.
* Lawmakers pushed for change, and Amendment I required a 55% vote to pass. The House Resolution was drafted in direct response to the 2023 Colorado Supreme Court decision ending capital punishment. With almost a two-thirds vote of the people, it gives judges the authority to hold first-degree murder defendants without bond when the evidence of guilt is strong.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[GET YOUR TICKET HERE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Colorado Christian camp sues state over gender facility access mandate](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Camp IdRaHaJe, a Christian summer camp in Colorado, is suing the state after being ordered to accommodate gender expression in facilities like bathrooms and sleeping quarters.
* Camp leaders argue the mandate violates their religious beliefs and mission, forcing them to choose between compliance and losing their license.
* The Alliance Defending Freedom is representing the camp, calling the state's policy an overreach that punishes faith-based institutions for holding biblical views on gender.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
** ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Gaines: Phil Weiser spends your tax dollars suing Trump and backing gun control](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY CORY GAINES****| COMMENTARY | MAY 20** _I was corresponding with someone recently and they mentioned something interesting. This person is a lawyer and said they were in a 10th circuit (Federal) courtroom recently and overheard an appellate case out of New Mexico about gun control._
__
**[Out of order: Was DNC vice chair David Hogg undone by procedure—or politics?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY JEN SCHUMANN | NATIONAL | MAY 19** _David Hogg says the Democratic National Committee (DNC) is trying to “fast-track” his removal as vice chair. The party insists it’s just following procedure. But veteran parliamentarian Gregory Carlson says what happened may violate one of the most basic principles of Robert’s Rules of Order – you can’t go back and undo a vote after the meeting is over._
__
**[The COvid Chronicles May 8–15, 2020: C&C made headlines. Polis made an example. Colorado made up its mind.](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD | COMMENTARY | MAY 18** _”The governor’s enforcers tried to make an example of C &C. Instead, they created a rallying cry. In just seven days, Colorado witnessed threats, shutdowns, viral videos and a surge of defiance that no press conference could contain. Counties revolted, small towns reopened and sheriffs made it clear: the edicts had lost their teeth._ __

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**🦁 DID YOU KNOW?** The **world’s largest zoo** is in Nebraska—really! Omaha’s Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium holds the title as the biggest AZA-accredited zoo in both the U.S. and the world, measured by acreage, species count, and exhibit size. Founded in 1895, it once showcased animals on loan from none other than Buffalo Bill himself.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value." – Albert Einstein

clean_body: Cities sue Polis over housing mandate, cite threat to local control | | | | PRESENTED BY | | Hi folks! Listen to today's Mountain Minute Podcast , or watch on or ! Here is a summary of today's top stories: 🏔️ Here in Colorado... Colorado’s soft-on-crime policies are under fire after a new report revealed violent offenders routinely commit new crimes . A Christian camp is over rules requiring access to gender identity–aligned facilities, calling it a violation of religious freedom. Meanwhile, multiple cities—including Arvada and Aurora—are over new housing mandates they say override local authority. And from gun control to gray wolves and wild bison, a slate of new bills passed this session will . Denver City Council is bracing for —but Mayor Mike Johnston still hasn’t revealed how deep they’ll go. 🇺🇸 On the National Stage... President Trump announced that Russia and Ukraine will begin immediate ceasefire negotiations following a , with the Vatican offering to host peace talks. Meanwhile, a sweeping new pro-family proposal includes —a 401(k)-style plan for newborns—as part of a larger push to boost the American birth rate and support young families. 🗣️ Commentary Spotlight... Cory Gaines blasts Colorado AG Phil Weiser for spending taxpayer money on and gun control efforts. Meanwhile, Dr. Brian Joondeph praises Trump’s , calling it a win for patients and a blow to global price disparities. This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, May 21, 2025. – Team RMV | TODAY'S TOP STORIES Six “home rule” cities in Colorado are suing the state, alleging it has unconstitutionally usurped their local authority over land use and zoning as it pushes communities to allow denser housing development. The lawsuit challenges the constitutionality of an executive order Gov. Jared Polis signed last week to withhold some state grants from local communities if they fail to implement a slate of recent housing laws. The cities say the order encroaches on the powers of both the General Assembly and the judiciary to say what the law is and is “beyond the governor’s authority.” The cities are also asking the courts to find they don’t have to follow two new laws approved by the legislature last year which require cities to allow denser housing near transit and to do away with minimum parking requirements for some new multi-family developments. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | A recent wave of violent crimes in Denver—committed by offenders out on low or no-cost bonds—has reignited debate over Colorado’s bail and sentencing policies. From a trooper nearly stabbed at the Capitol to a gas station shooting by a man with 50 prior arrests, public outrage is mounting. Lawmakers pushed for change, and Amendment I required a 55% vote to pass. The House Resolution was drafted in direct response to the 2023 Colorado Supreme Court decision ending capital punishment. With almost a two-thirds vote of the people, it gives judges the authority to hold first-degree murder defendants without bond when the evidence of guilt is strong. | | Camp IdRaHaJe, a Christian summer camp in Colorado, is suing the state after being ordered to accommodate gender expression in facilities like bathrooms and sleeping quarters. Camp leaders argue the mandate violates their religious beliefs and mission, forcing them to choose between compliance and losing their license. The Alliance Defending Freedom is representing the camp, calling the state's policy an overreach that punishes faith-based institutions for holding biblical views on gender. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ CORY GAINES| COMMENTARY | MAY 20 I was corresponding with someone recently and they mentioned something interesting. subject: Ban social media for teens under 16
body:
In-school smartphone bans are great. But the policies don't go far enough.

# [Ban social media for teens under 16](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=163715315&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzNzE1MzE1LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc4MzEwNTEsImV4cCI6MTc1MDQyMzA1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.l2d7uTvysXaDe-_t6u6-3AdEu64PnCIHwh3pm306rh0)

I [very](https://substack.com/redirect/bfd8a6df-7510-41e2-9c58-6b48d1efb3c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [consistently](https://substack.com/redirect/8db096ae-a594-499f-b366-879581f6a6a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [rail](https://substack.com/redirect/5e69c640-52a6-4ac9-a5af-79eeba314c3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against the Missouri Legislature. And to be clear, they deserve it.1
But I will give credit where it's due: the Legislature sent a bill to the governor that would [ban students from using cell phones during the school day](https://substack.com/redirect/08ac19d7-f226-40b9-9f6e-c00c77c71744?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
This is great policy, and the data is overwhelmingly clear that this is good for kids. But it doesn't go far enough: absent federal action, **states should ban social media use for minors under 16**.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTYzNzE1MzE1LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ3ODMxMDUxLCJleHAiOjE3NTA0MjMwNTEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.ELq2Hx1K814tVONvheQ3vwDqMaNodBIn8_O6R7v2ixE?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _States are right to ban students from using their phones during the school day (even outside of class)._
> * _Social media use is clearly bad for children, impacting everything from sleep to the incidence of depression to their literal brain chemistry._
> * _Since the federal government isn 't taking action, states should be more aggressive in banning children under 16 from using social media._
>

### The data confirms that phones in schools are very bad
This seems obvious: I don't need data to tell you that cell phones are distracting. I work very hard to not be addicted to my phone and I'm fully addicted to my phone.
In social situations, when we have a split-second of downtime, we pull out our phone. When the phones are out, we're less engaged in conversation, with our peers, and with the world.
But does the data back this up? Yes, emphatically--especially for students.
* [An analysis of nearly 150,000 students](https://substack.com/redirect/5d21f001-f463-44a5-ac30-c5c08e3ac6d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across 16 countries finds: "Smartphone addiction negatively impacts students' learning and overall academic performance.... The greater the use of a phone while studying, the greater the negative impact on learning and academic achievement."
* [According to a study](https://substack.com/redirect/4f944cfc-0aa2-4293-b1f7-3d22ffd1d071?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "smartphone bans reduced bullying rates in boys and girls, boosted female students' GPAs and resulted in fewer consultations for 'psychological symptoms and diseases among girls.'"
* [72% of high school teachers in America](https://substack.com/redirect/e0ac0bbc-b840-4514-bade-2394930be607?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) say that distraction from cell phones is "a major problem," and the [vast majority of teachers](https://substack.com/redirect/5603fe12-f4ad-4e3b-8fe5-6a105f068825?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) want to ban in-school phone use.
* "Results indicated that students whose smartphones were physically removed during class had higher levels of course comprehension, lower levels of anxiety, and higher levels of mindfulness than the control group," [according to a 2022 study](https://substack.com/redirect/d117908a-1b2c-49b1-bf37-f87ed54f2ea8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).2

Students themselves are [self-reporting improvements](https://substack.com/redirect/94acc695-5760-4499-bf9d-cec3dfe5c102?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in "grades, focus, and social interaction" when cell phone bans are implemented.3
Will students find ways around the rules? [Unquestionably](https://substack.com/redirect/130aedc3-5da2-4ff4-a095-c77a39630c50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But it's clear that across the board, smartphone bans work.4

### Post-COVID, the situation at schools is more dire
Boosting academic performance and student engagement are always worthy goals, but it's especially important post-pandemic.
* Teachers and educators are reporting [shocking increases in threats and violence](https://substack.com/redirect/6d670f89-bd2c-432e-a4b2-fd66eda67292?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since 2020.
* Students are [nowhere close to recovering learning losses](https://substack.com/redirect/efa07f64-d853-4f69-bbde-b1b3e08ccbb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the pandemic, and that's especially true for some of the [most at-risk students](https://substack.com/redirect/71a14111-97a4-4120-bece-2f6bdef079f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): homeless students, disabled students, English-language learners, and others.

These problems go far beyond smartphones, but they're clearly exacerbating the issue. It's no wonder that [in-class cell phone bans are so popular](https://substack.com/redirect/252b9978-7b35-4d42-83be-1e778add8fe6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across the U.S., and why so many states are moving in this direction.5
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/578ed737-b818-467b-862c-ed08cb6bdb91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

### Smartphone bans don't go far enough. Social media restrictions are essential
Ron DeSantis is right. (You won't hear me say that too often.) Last year, he signed a bill that [banned children under the age of 14](https://substack.com/redirect/6f282b81-069c-473d-ba38-d4ce2c6852ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from having social media accounts.
The ACLU [announced a lawsuit the same day](https://substack.com/redirect/6d1c1c5a-1d9f-415c-9f39-6afb7bd98ef9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), on First Amendment grounds. And there may well be some partisan motivations for DeSantis here, as the ACLU claims, but this is the wrong battle to pick: the data that social media is bad, especially for younger children, is unambiguous.
The baseline alone is jarring: teens spend, on average, [almost five hours per day](https://substack.com/redirect/216640e7-3381-4bb1-af1f-f8a2199169dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on social media--nearly one-third of their waking hours.6 The impact of that, unsurprisingly, is profound:
* "Adolescent social media use is associated with [higher rates](https://substack.com/redirect/3c458c8f-1a9d-4b79-8458-10ccec107e2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of anxiety and depression," as well as higher rates of eating disorders and sleep issues. ([Source](https://substack.com/redirect/578ed737-b818-467b-862c-ed08cb6bdb91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))
* The heaviest social media users have twice the risk of [negative mental health outcomes](https://substack.com/redirect/e3949ec7-9f8a-4883-a8f5-92686e53bba1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* **[48% of children ages 13-17 say](https://substack.com/redirect/db6298ae-fc6f-4e70-89de-43d3934308a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the impact of social media is "mostly negative." Only 11% say that it's "mostly positive."** They're on the front lines of this issue, and we should listen to them.
* Chasing "likes" on social media has negative impacts on developing teens and [literally changes their brain chemistry](https://substack.com/redirect/daf65d1d-7a9b-42d4-bb70-10e8534d5fad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), making children more sensitive and, paradoxically, [more compulsive social media users](https://substack.com/redirect/fd64a537-0c44-48bf-83f1-61c87c90b6ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This is especially important, because it points to the fact that the **impact of social media is causally negative** --we aren't just looking at correlations.7

**The solution here is to ban children under 16 from using social media**. Why 16? Because the [developmental impact is most profound before then](https://substack.com/redirect/3cfa7d36-c1b7-42c7-a47d-8aae265999f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and giving students a few years to use social media with some parental supervision before they turn 18 is a logical, if imperfect, compromise.
Is this paternalistic? Sure, but the government is paternalistic all the time when it comes to children.
We don't let [people under 21](https://substack.com/redirect/ce55931a-2b19-4c0c-99da-d416ab440b4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) smoke cigarettes.8 Most states [don't let people under 16](https://substack.com/redirect/93e3eb08-7f66-4f8c-b381-afa15e380a16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) drive cars. We don't let minors sign contracts or purchase guns or see R-rated movies alone.
We do this because the government has an obligation to protect children, and this is an instance where the data is overwhelming that the status quo isn't working.9
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=163715315&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzNzE1MzE1LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc4MzEwNTEsImV4cCI6MTc1MDQyMzA1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.l2d7uTvysXaDe-_t6u6-3AdEu64PnCIHwh3pm306rh0)
### I'm not a Luddite, but the data here is clear
I'm not anti-technology.10 Innovation in science has led to [remarkable gains](https://substack.com/redirect/af474c4f-dece-4e97-a772-71e6518d6de6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in life expectancy and public health. I'm a serial ChatGPT user--it's rare that I _don 't_ use ChatGPT when I write, either to help with research or to help me proofread.11
But: that doesn't mean we should allow advances in technology to go unchecked.
Take car safety as an example. The death rate from car crashes [has been in decline for decades](https://substack.com/redirect/4f0173cb-f84b-4eba-a091-78bfd005045c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [especially among children](https://substack.com/redirect/076d2365-65ac-4772-a933-e35d2f522b1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Why? Because of changes in policy: since they were implemented, seat belt laws [have saved hundreds of thousands of lives](https://substack.com/redirect/8a93394b-2c55-4631-a228-11cd0ad5bdcb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). So have [airbags and backup cameras](https://substack.com/redirect/13037785-cd6f-4298-87a3-ec8b3822417a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and other technologies.12
In an era where too many children are struggling with loneliness, lagging academic performance (since COVID-19 school shutdowns), and unhappiness, the solutions here are clear: **policymakers should be aggressive in banning phones from schools and banning social media use for minors under 16**.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTYzNzE1MzE1LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ3ODMxMDUxLCJleHAiOjE3NTA0MjMwNTEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.ELq2Hx1K814tVONvheQ3vwDqMaNodBIn8_O6R7v2ixE?)

1
Just last week:
* They sent a [ballot measure to ban abortion](https://substack.com/redirect/e5ff28ba-4932-4a20-ab19-744e5b24f504?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) back to the voters, even though voters just legalized it via constitutional amendment in [the election last November](https://substack.com/redirect/69012a7e-7b8b-4836-9899-b39a5c0e32d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* They [repealed worker protection laws](https://substack.com/redirect/8be24cd3-38d0-4bc7-8fa7-129b0747840a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), even though voters overwhelmingly passed those protections at the ballot in [that same election](https://substack.com/redirect/95844485-fa98-4a80-a2c0-ac04f44aec60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

2
The evidence may be less robust, but in-school phone bans lead to better behavior. At one school in Connecticut, a [robust smartphone ban](https://substack.com/redirect/4f944cfc-0aa2-4293-b1f7-3d22ffd1d071?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) led to a "30% to 40% decline in suspensions at the school."
In general, the [presence of a smartphone increases anxiety](https://substack.com/redirect/ebdb7a23-cc69-42ea-8b60-2acc2ae7eb03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the median student spends [more than 10% of their school day](https://substack.com/redirect/e3949ec7-9f8a-4883-a8f5-92686e53bba1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on their phone. (The math here: [43 minutes per student per day](https://substack.com/redirect/464ceaa0-46b7-4eb6-9916-64fec9620473?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the average school day is [6.64 hours](https://substack.com/redirect/067bb005-9ddf-4bb1-97ea-be7a818a1bae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S.)
Of course, this means that some students are spending considerably more time on their phones--and I'm guessing, based on all of the other research, that students struggling socially and/or academically are spending even more time than that on their phones.
3
One of the [very few arguments](https://substack.com/redirect/94acc695-5760-4499-bf9d-cec3dfe5c102?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) I've seen against cell phone bans from students is exceedingly weak: "Older students were more likely to acknowledge the benefits [of cell phone bans] but emphasized the importance of capturing memories like homecoming events with their phone cameras."
A few thoughts here:
* We were able to take pictures just fine in a pre-smartphone era.
* Fewer pictures is probably just fine.
* [Disposable](https://substack.com/redirect/1be24e16-08e3-40f0-b785-e68c58c2c981?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [cameras](https://substack.com/redirect/ca70cc83-f4ac-47a2-ad3c-27ad4fd628b4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [are](https://substack.com/redirect/d8872b32-6605-4308-93af-5dfc2bcde7f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [back](https://substack.com/redirect/a88877ec-8504-4fe0-8294-c761aa2cdcb4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!

So, this argument holds exactly zero merit in my mind.
4
Oddly enough, one of the few groups that's less in favor of these sorts of bans? [Parents](https://substack.com/redirect/bc70f391-36a4-45cc-a4ff-36f9f9a85448?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who've grown accustomed to knowing where their children are all the time, and being able to reach them anytime and anywhere. (Of course, parenthood worked just fine in an era before you were able to reach your kid immediately at school.)
5
This map is already slightly out of date--namely, Missouri is missing--but it paints a good picture of what's happening nationally. Bans are adopted in blue states, in purple states, and in red states. This isn't a partisan issue.
6
Teens need about [nine hours of sleep per night](https://substack.com/redirect/6666d0ca-4c18-44b5-8350-acc3818b409e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), according to this Johns Hopkins data, though that obviously varies a ton from child to child.
7
Said differently: we can't just make the argument that lonelier people are more likely to be using social media in the first place. It's an extremely compelling argument that social media is actually making people lonelier.
8
Social media companies have been [compared to the tobacco industry](https://substack.com/redirect/178d69c1-33f2-4ab1-a9bd-8dc6bf30c48e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But it goes further than that: just for good measure, frequent social media use [leads to increases in tobacco and e-cigarette use](https://substack.com/redirect/f47c1a38-4b56-415e-ae7b-a53c152e432a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
9
On top of everything else: despite its name ("social" media), people who spend [more time on social media are significantly lonelier](https://substack.com/redirect/dc152ea1-571c-42e9-915b-51d173811ffe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As I've [written](https://substack.com/redirect/270501eb-f337-4307-950d-4311c9120761?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [about](https://substack.com/redirect/03d2c01e-4ec0-4535-b1c8-01eb550535da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [before](https://substack.com/redirect/1b029894-8c88-451a-980d-c72a109dc67c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), loneliness is one of the pressing issues of our time.
10
I love that the term "Luddite" comes from a [19th-century English political movement](https://substack.com/redirect/5bbb4193-2d3c-4617-9398-9a79b447b2d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that mobilized against advances in technology. (And with the acceleration of AI, I wouldn't at all be surprised to see [neo-Luddism](https://substack.com/redirect/5d48247d-560c-40c0-9db1-efbc9a95bcea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) emerge as a movement.)
11
Nor, by the way, am I trying to be Mrs. Helen Lovejoy from _The Simpsons_ , wailing on behalf of the children:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/945cee10-7e37-4b94-afff-ebd6152d307b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
12
What this points to: this isn't _solely_ the responsibility of government. Advances in technology led by car companies have unquestionably saved lives too. There's a lesson in here for social media companies.

---

clean_body: In-school smartphone bans are great. But the policies don't go far enough. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more In-school smartphone bans are great. But the policies don't go far enough. | | | May 21 | | | | | | | | | | | | I against the Missouri Legislature. And to be clear, they deserve it.1 But I will give credit where it's due: the Legislature sent a bill to the governor that would . This is great policy, and the data is overwhelmingly clear that this is good for kids. But it doesn't go far enough: absent federal action, states should ban social media use for minors under 16. ? Article summary: States are right to ban students from using their phones during the school day (even outside of class). Social media use is clearly bad for children, impacting everything from sleep to the incidence of depression to their literal brain chemistry. Since the federal government isn 't taking action, states should be more aggressive in banning children under 16 from using social media. The data confirms that phones in schools are very bad This seems obvious: I don't need data to tell you that cell phones are distracting. I work very hard to not be addicted to my phone and I'm fully addicted to my phone. In social situations, when we have a split-second of downtime, we pull out our phone. When the phones are out, we're less engaged in conversation, with our peers, and with the world. But does the data back this up? Yes, emphatically--especially for students. across 16 countries finds: "Smartphone addiction negatively impacts students' learning and overall academic performance.... The greater the use of a phone while studying, the greater the negative impact on learning and academic achievement." , "smartphone bans reduced bullying rates in boys and girls, boosted female students' GPAs and resulted in fewer consultations for 'psychological symptoms and diseases among girls.'" say that distraction from cell phones is "a major problem," and the want to ban in-school phone use. "Results indicated that students whose smartphones were physically removed during class had higher levels of course comprehension, lower levels of anxiety, and higher levels of mindfulness than the control group," Students themselves are in "grades, focus, and social interaction" when cell phone bans are implemented.3 Will students find ways around the rules? . But it's clear that across the board, smartphone bans work.4 Post-COVID, the situation at schools is more dire Boosting academic performance and student engagement are always worthy goals, but it's especially important post-pandemic. Teachers and educators are reporting since 2020. Students are from the pandemic, and that's especially true for some of the : homeless students, disabled students, English-language learners, and others. These problems go far beyond smartphones, but they're clearly exacerbating the issue. It's no wonder that across the U.S., and why so many states are moving in this direction.5 | | ---|---|--- Smartphone bans don't go far enough. Social media restrictions are essential Ron DeSantis is right. (You won't hear me say that too often.) Last year, he signed a bill that from having social media accounts. The ACLU , on First Amendment grounds. And there may well be some partisan motivations for DeSantis here, as the ACLU claims, but this is the wrong battle to pick: the data that social media is bad, especially for younger children, is unambiguous. The baseline alone is jarring: teens spend, on average, on social media--nearly one-third of their waking hours.6 The impact of that, unsurprisingly, is profound: "Adolescent social media use is associated with of anxiety and depression," as well as higher rates of eating disorders and sleep issues. () The heaviest social media users have twice the risk of . that the impact of social media is "mostly negative." Only 11% say that it's "mostly positive." They're on the front lines of this issue, and we should listen to them. Chasing "likes" on social media has negative impacts on developing teens and , making children more sensitive and, paradoxically, . This is especially important, because it points to the fact that the impact of social media is causally negative --we aren't just looking at correlations.7 The solution here is to ban children under 16 from using social media. Why 16? Because the , and giving students a few years to use social media with some parental supervision before they turn 18 is a logical, if imperfect, compromise. Is this paternalistic? Sure, but the government is paternalistic all the time when it comes to children. We don't let smoke cigarettes.8 Most states drive cars. We don't let minors sign contracts or purchase guns or see R-rated movies alone. We do this because the government has an obligation to protect children, and this is an instance where the data is overwhelming that the status quo isn't working.9 I'm not a Luddite, but the data here is clear I'm not anti-technology.10 Innovation in science has led to in life expectancy and public health. I'm a serial ChatGPT user--it's rare that I don 't use ChatGPT when I write, either to help with research or to help me proofread.11 But: that doesn't mean we should allow advances in technology to go unchecked. Take car safety as an example. The death rate from car crashes , . Why? Because of changes in policy: since they were implemented, seat belt laws . So have and other technologies.12 In an era where too many children are struggling with loneliness, lagging academic performance (since COVID-19 school shutdowns), and unhappiness, the solutions here are clear: policymakers should be aggressive in banning phones from schools and banning social media use for minors under 16. ? (The math here: , and the average school day is in the U.S.) Of course, this means that some students are spending considerably more time on their phones--and I'm guessing, based on all of the other research, that students struggling socially and/or academically are spending even more time than that on their phones. 3 One of the I've seen against cell phone bans from students is exceedingly weak: "Older students were more likely to acknowledge the benefits [of cell phone bans] but emphasized the importance of capturing memories like homecoming events with their phone cameras." A few thoughts here: We were able to take pictures just fine in a pre-smartphone era. Fewer pictures is probably just fine. ! So, this argument holds exactly zero merit in my mind. 4 Oddly enough, one of the few groups that's less in favor of these sorts of bans? , who've grown accustomed to knowing where their children are all the time, and being able to reach them anytime and anywhere. name: Laura Loomer
subject: After I visited the Oval Office . . .
body:
I was invited to the Oval Office for a meeting. I brought a list of National Security Council staff who were sabotaging Trump's agenda and who had been disloyal to him.
| If this is irrelevant to you, please click **[unsubscribe here](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Funsubscribe%2FwJCYyn7638HXOcoR8928MiNxO5nXTgGo6s7D8pT0763BW8929so%2FjNrcjifZkRuM7634SQt2EHxg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/1/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/H_j3I3CvzGRXYUnzATRF6X22_OA=427)** to be removed.
* * *
---
| [****](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FNdN3hhQog0IxH3oXyipWjg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/1/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/KkVBdxEz9yEk8Z7xJo5LVvXN6y4=427) [****](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FNdN3hhQog0IxH3oXyipWjg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/2/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/kBXzwKU2K9fugLj--qPghV9K8wg=427) Dear Patriot,

The DC swamp is crawling with vipers--left-wing loons and right-wing frauds--**who view President Trump 's White House as their personal ATM or fame factory.**

These snakes don't care about making America great again. They're after power, money, or a glossy magazine cover. **I 'm on a mission to help rip these profiteers out of President Trump's orbit** and surround him with loyal patriots who live for this country, not their gain.

[**Please help me keep going by donating to support my work.**](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FNdN3hhQog0IxH3oXyipWjg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/3/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/KxMlV5hxHh5MwsPkSH7YPeoJk5A=427)

In April, **I was invited to the Oval Office for a meeting**. I brought a list of National Security Council staff who were sabotaging Trump's agenda and who had been disloyal to him.

These weren't warriors--they were careerists playing Trump, hoping they would never be questioned or removed despite their record of not supporting President Trump..

After my meeting, **President Trump immediately fired six of them, and several weeks later, he replaced his National Security Advisor, Mike Waltz.**

**This is what I do** : expose the snakes and fight for patriots, because if we don't enforce loyalty tests under the new admin, then we will see a repeat of the first term, where the White House was sabotaged from within at the expense of the American people.

[**I would like you to donate to support my investigative reports and help me continue exposing disloyalty in the Swamp.**](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FNdN3hhQog0IxH3oXyipWjg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/4/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/GHvOoZzafoTYS25N7D3GXn34K8o=427)

The Swamp fights back, and I'm battling it alone, paying for my flights, hotels, and research to expose people who seek to derail the Trump administration. I could use your help to keep going. [**Your support keeps me and my team in the trenches.**](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FNdN3hhQog0IxH3oXyipWjg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/5/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/mfBkuAIPdFwcCn43Jxz_m2xPS90=427)

[**Please donate $25, $50, or $100 now to help me continue my investigations into career bureaucrats who seek to bamboozle the American people and sabotage our President.**](https://5gcvd67r.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Flibersend.loomered.com%2Fl%2FbZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA%2FNdN3hhQog0IxH3oXyipWjg%2FUyZZlxcFja7ccb1etsdAuQ/6/01000196f2e26f5b-76b50cc2-f311-435e-a267-8371c9abef9c-000000/X6O3HaOuq4aYjuGr-hf81ed8xKI=427)

Thank you so much for all that you do.

With Vigilance, Laura Loomer

body:

| **_OFFICIAL MEMO FROM THE DESK OF THE HOUSE GOP LEADERSHIP CHAIR, REP. ELISE STEFANIK_**
---
Peter,
---
The generosity of grassroots conservatives never ceases to amaze me.
---

Inflation is Rising – Relief for Families
Is On the Line

SUPPORT THE BIG, BEAUTIFUL BILL:
Secure the Child Tax Credit for
Working Families

Join MFA President Kimberly Fletcher, MFA Vice President Debbie Kraulidis, Political Director Emily Stack, and Deputy Director
Raquel Mitchell to learn why the Child Tax Credit (CTC) must be prioritized in the upcoming reconciliation bill.

We’ll break down the policy, the polling, and the path forward – featuring top voices in the pro-family movement and real moms who
are feeling the pressure at the kitchen table.

WATCH THE VIDEO [http://email.mg.momsforamerica.us/c/eJxsUk1zozoQ_DVwiwsE4uOgg-0Fr-PdrB1jm_jiEvoAGSQRIfA6v_6Vk1ev3iHH6ZqZnu5pchEUlXzqfGrhBr8Xf3gZn8_p5FLE_ZiGvku1xEIhWc-klgPXBktmBMGzcXAZ8uMwToIQQOgyiUV3kWwYcM0ee7ebZfUrOk5JuQk9eK2bA7P_dtl7zxDBsseiVv_DHmNRAgglvPJBxDwSpIzTitOEuUxNwmglmbKoN5qOxAqt3AYB6CUVZKkPKcCYepSlVeVHKfGCFNIgdQUCHoAeBL4fBB4MZ0Hg-5xBgCn1WJByJ_S-Fdihxtp-cIK5A3IH5J1Q7UwO9aComREtHZB_Hv9kDSatULUD8thPQRxTEJPECXKilcXErqkT_Nht7O28yz4WV2NAcW1ff92l25HLl36hLDMKd-gA8oGujm3ppz_P6e3t9fie4ykO1vAqgm2ZLRyw2PfZ6q09_Ti8bUew3ubhkd5JPa3DRlo5X6Wt9yxWf1M4b7ihdN3Myz_7DBbp3TogP70uNVgOyXMIo5fuxeIPM-eTVB6OeJI_n4uSZ1l4PgXwNqyWzbD2C8728S6q1MhPh_Ja8G58xz95Mjz_dcBiu0juxfb3-CJiBywKSrI8_rju3E5_5qu3KuqzvTyuwt9x1F9DtmSuZLbRFOFeuNqIWihUjV379GmF2xs9CcoMelT1qFyDaH8TXSeGKHZCr37gD__dQY-GMPT1BDFMTzdtWmZcix7k3zJbRLS8fBt6i5i9_BfLCYF_AgAA___oVg2V]

If you care about protecting families, preserving conservative values, and making sure Washington hears from moms, don’t miss
this.

Why the CTC Matters:

Inflation is squeezing working families – groceries, gas, and childcare all cost more. As moms we know first-hand how it’s
impacting our wallets at home. The updated Child Tax Credit (CTC) provisions in the reconciliation bill are a commonsense,
conservative solution that helps parents keep more of what they earn.

What's in the Bill:

* Raises the CTC to $2,500 per child through 2028

* Makes the $2,000 base permanent starting in 2029 and indexes it to inflation

* Keeps refundability, providing real relief to lower-income working families

* Requires valid Social Security numbers, ensuring the credit goes to eligible households

Rooted in Conservative Values:

* First introduced in the Contract with America

* Pro-family, pro-work, and pro-growth

* Returns tax dollars to parents, not bureaucrats

* Strengthens households without expanding government

What Congress Should Do:

* Pass the reconciliation bill with these critical CTC provisions

clean_body: Support the Child Tax Credit in the Reconciliation Bill – Support Working Families If you are having trouble viewing this email click here to view in your browser. [] [] [] Inflation is Rising – Relief for Families Is On the Line SUPPORT THE BIG, BEAUTIFUL BILL: Secure the Child Tax Credit for Working Families Join MFA President Kimberly Fletcher, MFA Vice President Debbie Kraulidis, Political Director Emily Stack, and Deputy Director Raquel Mitchell to learn why the Child Tax Credit (CTC) must be prioritized in the upcoming reconciliation bill. We’ll break down the policy, the polling, and the path forward – featuring top voices in the pro-family movement and real moms who are feeling the pressure at the kitchen table. WATCH THE VIDEO [] If you care about protecting families, preserving conservative values, and making sure Washington hears from moms, don’t miss this. Why the CTC Matters: Inflation is squeezing working families – groceries, gas, and childcare all cost more. As moms we know first-hand how it’s impacting our wallets at home. The updated Child Tax Credit (CTC) provisions in the reconciliation bill are a commonsense, conservative solution that helps parents keep more of what they earn. What's in the Bill: Raises the CTC to $2,500 per child through 2028 Makes the $2,000 base permanent starting in 2029 and indexes it to inflation Keeps refundability, providing real relief to lower-income working families Requires valid Social Security numbers, ensuring the credit goes to eligible households Rooted in Conservative Values: First introduced in the Contract with America Pro-family, pro-work, and pro-growth Returns tax dollars to parents, not bureaucrats Strengthens households without expanding government What Congress Should Do: Pass the reconciliation bill with these critical CTC provisions Reinforce support for working families who are building America’s future Protect a tax credit that empowers parents and respects the dignity of work Let's get it done – this is the Big, Beautiful Bill working families deserve. * Click here for more information and to watch the FULL VIDEO SERIES [] TAKE ACTION NOW!!! Add Your Name to our Petition to Urge Action From Your Representatives SIGN THE PETITION TODAY! [] Thank you for taking a stand for faith, family, and freedom. Thank you for your continued commitment to our mission. [] In Truth & Liberty, Kimberly Fletcher Founder and President Moms for America Action® [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] Copyright © 2025 Moms for America, All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: Moms for America Action 1440 State HWY 248, Suite Q178 Branson, MO 65616 Visit our Website [] Visit our Store [] Visit us on Truth Social [] DONATE TODAY []

name: Mesoscale News with Rebekah Jones
subject: Limited time: Save 75% off paid subscription
body:
While prices for monthly access across Substack are being raised, I'm dropping mine.

| |
---|---|---
|
# Limited time: Save 75% off paid subscription
| | May 21
---|---
While prices for monthly access across Substack are being raised, I'm dropping mine.
I incur some costs in the work I do, but I have always believed in making information accessible when it's critical for the public to know.
That's why I'm dropping the price of my monthly and annual subscriptions for new subscribers.
The average Substack subscription costs $8 per month.
**[I'm dropping mine to $1.75 per month, or $17.50 for one year. ](https://substack.com/redirect/e92cb792-c879-4315-bd9a-669403feccdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Click here to subscribe!](https://substack.com/redirect/e92cb792-c879-4315-bd9a-669403feccdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I've struggled to pay for my own subscriptions over the last few years, and I certainly don't want to be a source of stress for anyone who wants to support my work, especially now as families like mine are struggling.
And with the changes happening within our country, I don't want to add another burden to the long list of troubles in store for all of us.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3f53d19b-fbbc-4896-96bc-f0c86b7cabf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
If you're willing and able to give more, I'd ask that you[ either donate to my lawsuit](https://substack.com/redirect/98dbed78-eb8b-4fe7-bdbc-adb94f4a35e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or donate to[ Project Zero.](https://substack.com/redirect/ad9d3d0a-e60e-4339-9d0e-1cf4d5b8e85c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
From now until June 21, you can save 10% off Project Zero shirts and tote bags using the code "CORAL2025." [Click here to shop](https://substack.com/redirect/545c6e59-79dd-4574-ad3a-b2528930b7ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!
I would rather more people have access to all of my work for a lower price, than only a few hundred have access for a higher price.
Thank you continuing to support my work!
(C) 2025 Rebekah Jones
PO BOX 3371, Silver Spring, MD 20918
[Unsubscribe](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.V8OcPKtbibxh27jyMfLo0rkB3l0RiIsWC7_Qjbryrm0?)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/82d48486-63bd-4617-aa26-63e10d9abc85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[](https://substack..ZL2TUroIAqSxeU_yvJvCyB0zfOa7Ord9pQwBsncDyCU?)

We have a MAJOR endorsement to share today: **Ghazala Hashmi — one of the leading candidates in the Democratic primary for Virginia Lieutenant Governor! She is the ONLY woman running against five men in the primary, and is the most experienced legislator in her race.**

This seat is currently held by a far-right, anti-choice Republican named Winsome Earle-Sears who is now the Republican frontrunner for Governor.

**_Hashmi has a history of overcoming the odds._** When she first ran for office, she surprised political pundits when she defeated the party establishment’s hand-picked candidate for the nomination. She then went on to win in the general election in a swing district that had been represented by a Republican for decades – by TEN points. As a result, Democrats gained a critical one-seat majority in the Chamber, and **Hashmi was able to cast a tie-breaking vote to enact abortion rights into law.**

Hashmi was motivated to run for office initially in opposition to Trump, and has challenged the Trump and Youngkin agenda in the General Assembly ever since.

**If she wins the primary election, Hashmi will face off against an extremist Republican talk-radio host, John Reid.** His campaign website features MAGA-inspired rhetoric against Democrats, how he wants to “free education from leftist indoctrination,” stop “wokeness,” and continue “his fight against boys in girls sports and the extreme trans-agenda being forced upon our children.” Additionally, he had made it clear that he is anti-choice.

His campaign website states, **“Who better to make our conservative moral case than a man like John Reid?”**

Virginia is a competitive state – and Hashmi’s primary is no joke. It will take all of us to ensure that we win this seat, take back the executive offices, and protect and expand the pro-choice majority in the House of Delegates. We are so proud to stand with Senator Hashmi in this fight.

[**Hashmi has taken on the MAGA agenda before, and now she’s ready to do it again as Lieutenant Governor. Help us ensure her TV ad can reach as many voters as possible by rushing $25 or more today →**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hbm-hashmi/?refcode=EM_F_250521hashmi_N_EH_90DO_11_G_TestA_L2&amount=25)

Together,

Erica Henry
Senior Political Advisor

name: Chris Taylor for Justice
subject: It’s official: Judge Chris Taylor is running for the Wisconsin Supreme Court
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
This race isn’t just about two different judges — it’s about two very different visions for Wisconsin’s future.
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7c077b90-89c4-4879-a0e9-e429ad4a7488/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Breaking news: Judge Chris Taylor is running for the Wisconsin Supreme Court. [Before we say more, can you split $10 between this campaign and Blue Amp Action? We’re counting on grassroots support to win.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7e1927a0-e137-409e-9d6d-696c5bc11079/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Peter, Chris has spent her entire career fighting for our freedoms. As a lawyer, public servant, and now as a judge, she’s never backed down from a tough fight. Today, Chris is stepping up once again — because our rights, our democracy, and our courts are under attack. Our opponent, Rebecca Bradley, embodies the division and extremism threatening everything we care about. Bradley has sided time and again with right-wing special interests, opposing reproductive freedom, undermining workers’ rights, and threatening our democracy. She has turned her back on Wisconsinites. This race isn’t just about two different judges, Peter. It’s about two very different visions for Wisconsin’s future and the importance of a fair and impartial Wisconsin Supreme Court. If we’re going to compete with the right-wing money machine, we need a massive show of grassroots strength. During the last Wisconsin Supreme Court election, Elon Musk spent over $20 million trying to buy a seat on our Supreme Court. If Elon Musk and other right-wing special interests get involved again, we need to be ready for everything they’ll throw at us. That’s why we’re asking: [Can you become a founding donor by splitting a donation of $10 between our campaign and Blue Amp Action now?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T48b170e2-a604-4da7-85fa-eb8175000666/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE $10](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta917fcec-4fe1-4b16-a25e-8a810f0a99e0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | Thank you, Team Taylor
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
|
---
| | |
---
| | | | |
---

| **Blue Amp Action PAC**
PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208
---
| Having trouble viewing this email? [View it in your web browser](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/loc_em_fr_20250521_taylor_b2_f1_s1_c1_92op_n-t1?wvpId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | |
---
| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T190e2552-8ac2-44e4-a249-69682abd338e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: President Trump
subject: God spared my life for a reason.

name: Gary Sinise
subject: These heroes are counting on you
body:
Help me celebrate 20 years of the National Memorial Day Parade| | | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq3xu_ngNv43MPqeOlJoBL4JOmCvCq01pd3uROqQVprLGjDgnTz3dwmU0-Lrgj1GU07A9fJXSIlesXZ-F5YlPpL3380bJwG01HF5bhxBYEmoCusQhJQcnxhiGSgTgBxGAKJ1MnW7d5doQk8vewqXY07t)
---
Peter, It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 __and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **Now, I'm grateful to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal on the 20th anniversary of this important event.** This year will be a remembrance like no other, as we commemorate the 80th anniversary of victory in World War II and the 250th birthdays of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps. **[Peter, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENTIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous Americans like you. Will you help chip in today to help support the Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq3sisBD2uuJZqx59iKmOGIzbmocBg_9XG-ETSR7rClrdQ0PHrMBUuNqcN2Mt5XalL5AMLE_Gjjq9ymNNzMaajoSBOtUTcG6OAT7WFhkHQWJHXl6GVm0B8wMy7nLz7BwYlaM0-lL2PFnaV-I7n_kS6TD)**
---
[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq27F9L6lmICNkYGpuU5SZx1tcmq7dbODWKNavroC9UHQ0s1qHm9NJDeccFX5KSvsE-zN1du-v3tQENMBghkFOIYH8CIK52C-CCJK_F-op0PUfO6hpVfzIXaaujrCa1g3Gyr264qO77azPV0hK5XrKNp)
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq26x5wwSss8i3p6ScUqJbtimcowow5__0fE3LZMNUz4Ae7dqhk2-4wF-5XFrkWJKO9mk8-hQqp1ARZbO28MGUtVKhQbmCmAawFzOHZtVTG87sAAi6W7uSE0gKF7VaYcge9xCBZw-nXiF4GN8tIWbO33)
---
The National Memorial Day Parade costs a fraction of other major public events to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that 's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ **But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded _entirely_ by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country.** Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the Parade, but also supports great programs like:
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to Today before we lose these stories forever.

* Hold our National Youth Summit which allows young students a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, and Vietnam.

* Produce veteran focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-y3Vp4xH-9vJdo5hjwgZg-BO4ApJxrvGQ3JWM9tFf--hlj2-lQF0L6rv5ZjFJ5g0xnAqirEtmcBC7eqocQC3JVbBUAVbv9UkP_ezX3adqc5_safJaFTvEYJJ96XmTeC-8J4f6nDLocUCDgm_gIstjbW14OF785eqjjkg13bKpEfByrZ4zMSgdWUn6V52OtcwX3qr3rlyyA7-1yOReUwYkGDvifBchP5DqNX8Jdem1dN48%3D)[**Please join me in support of the National Memorial Day Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, or more right now.**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq10pSPakFLs-3ZToa_7l6ywJrqLblDOzO6afBQIqNpJK84o_T-JgbjYl3Oq_D9r6ILbEpP5z0xFp_lK33zYUpSbC9nz1ijapWmTKEs3R35RCx2b9EFhI8Ux2yMXKWyJwJDMSyHIPRCrkWTabSMHvJeH) If you've been looking for an opportunity to give back to those who have given so much for our freedom, this is one of the best possible ways you could do it. _And for a special group of veterans, this might be one of the last chances we get._ You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult. WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. _But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade._** ** **That's why we are making a concerted effort to ensure these veterans are at this year's National Memorial Day Parade to honor them one last time. ** So this year, as we mark the 80th anniversary of victory in Europe and Japan to end World War II - and the 250th anniversaries of the Army, Navy, and Marine Corps - I encourage you to help us honor these brave veterans while we still have them with us.
---
[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq2_m9qfUAAtROyzX2peIbSbFmNSshiKkEl_Mv_c-iou7JB452JTis917brGqmE0JSO4Zz9b5_1JqfNXn8LMSgKsZ5_S65dug4KXytoAlAOT9X4xVwhJ0Ju5bajM6nGOk34SpySnFeXQpfy3g7GBkf0K)
---
And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. _I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more._ _**[Peter, thank you for your support of America's heroes, and please - if you're able, make a gift to fund the National Memorial Day Parade right now during its 20th anniversary.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq2pwsonV4UVI5sFL4CTkMHKDUoKB_Dr8TicL10x-IABSckPmfUlHtf7x-QXNQegXb6vvuRChZVtz8agVpHqEj2KV7Gh7QZxVMsGDeW2IpsEJV9xhXglwgIoHa5SEGPT6ejZ0QpPoG7gkX7v2UVtkgzG)**_ Sincerely, Gary SiniseHonorary Grand MarshalNational Memorial Day Parade
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq2aRpSHPZcYsjmFIDBWg0knoWDs542yu6dcxj_Eh0qx9D5iHQ4SXfCto_q_KKi_eB7zKsP9ND3rVpR3YHN20M55dF0nsHJDxmdZSsBTXMBLKJUJEKrUiexOWvTiGXaKObwhZtuhz0nMBfUfVX2WbKMe)
---
[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=279158c4899e4b9abdb3f708b38c53c1&_e=1IUzoOeZVbYL-XLaYeUH87y7AR2AbRWb_ZGiDUkPRe5I0IUiIFgVW0YgcUdlNh-yF3uQoKC4Qp9-T2zyJw0llZCvak3a0NZm7w_16Et1YJyBreAwcpD3glEjKril34RR-K9LP2NQFFjJsHBnLhEOq14suZ98f9t25sBVkyzCvq1idUYeC2tah6a4SPHoL2mgrB1ECdI-dNi3P3uS3CfpGOCdWyar5iKMUd65hZRGpk-OO-jZvb46VnINimJEtsGS7g_2Nt_vnqXD1tS8fTZz8V2Nq9XpQ2681LUzipV7YulFo3YVJuqj6aUl3cNPlDQs)
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult. WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade. That's why we are making a concerted effort to ensure these veterans are at this year's National Memorial Day Parade to honor them one last time. So this year, as we mark the 80th anniversary of victory in Europe and Japan to end World War II - and the 250th anniversaries of the Army, Navy, and Marine Corps - I encourage you to help us honor these brave veterans while we still have them with us. And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. ** Sincerely, Gary SiniseHonorary Grand MarshalNational Memorial Day Parade The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
